content.description:
A type definition describes a category of items. Types are themselves items, creating a self-describing system.

## Type Chains

Every item's `type` field points to a type item. Type items use `extends` to form inheritance chains:

```
my-note → note → kernel:item
my-view → kernel:view → kernel:code → kernel:item
```

Chains always terminate at [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000), which has `extends: null`.

The kernel validates type chains on every save, rejecting:
- Missing type references
- Circular chains
- Chains that don't reach kernel:item

## The `extends` Field

Type definitions use `extends` to establish inheritance:

```javascript
{
  id: "note-type-uuid",
  name: "note",
  type: "11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",  // This is a type definition
  extends: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", // Extends kernel:item
  content: {
    description: "A markdown note",
    required_fields: ["description"]
  }
}
```

## View Dispatch

When rendering an item, the system walks its type chain looking for a matching [view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000):

1. Check item's `preferredView`
2. Check type's `preferredView`
3. Walk the type chain via `extends`, find view with matching `for_type`
4. Fall back to default view (JSON inspector)

This means a view for `kernel:item` works for *all* items (it's at the root). A view for `note` only works for notes and subtypes of note.

## Creating a New Type

```javascript
await api.set({
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: "my-custom-type",
  type: api.IDS.TYPE_DEFINITION,  // "11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  extends: api.IDS.ITEM,          // "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  attachments: [],
  content: {
    description: "Describe what this type represents",
    required_fields: ["title"]  // Optional: hint for editors
  }
});
```

No restart needed—items of the new type immediately work with existing views.

## Seed Types

| Type | Extends | Purpose |
|------|---------|--------|
| [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | null | Root of all types |
| [kernel:type-definition](item://11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | kernel:item | Types themselves |
| [kernel:code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | kernel:item | Executable items |
| [kernel:library](item://66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | kernel:code | Reusable modules |
| [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | kernel:code | Rendering code |

## See Also

- [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — The universal item structure
- [kernel:view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — How types get rendered
